title: EvidenceBundleManifest
description: Closed manifest of the ordered evidence entries that back a DiD experiment outcome (the reproducible evidence bundle — ALG §3.7-3:196, §11.3:674-676; k3s Gate C:540). The manifest is an ORDERED list of content-addressed entries (registration, deployment confirmation, baseline/treatment/control observations, DiD inputs/outputs, B-gate decision, GRS policy, missingness/remediation/rollback) plus the bundle's own manifest_hash. PRIVACY (wave5-plan.md non-scope, H10): entries carry content HASHES and object REFS ONLY — never raw customer query/content/screenshots/secrets inline. Tenant scoping is carried by the surrounding context/envelope (ADR-0013/ADR-0024(e)), NOT re-projected into this record.
$comment: compat_class: closed (additionalProperties:false at root and on every entry, like all domain records), signed: false (internal evidence index, not a human-signature artifact). Authority: docs/architecture/wave5-plan.md deliverable 4 (evidence bundle) + E5 (complete + tamper/reorder/splice-evident; no raw customer content/secrets). The entries[] ORDER is significant (reorder-evident): the manifest_hash is computed over the canonical serialization of the ordered entries (reuse saena_domain.audit canonical hashing — wave5-plan.md 'no new hashing rule'), so any reorder/splice changes manifest_hash. entry.content_hash uses the common/identifiers sha256_ref shape ('sha256:'-prefixed lowercase hex) for catalog consistency — every hash in this catalog is sha256_ref-shaped; entry.ref uses uri_ref (query/fragment forbidden — presigned-token smuggling defense, ADR-0024 R9-5). kind enum enumerates exactly the evidence-bundle component classes the DiD pipeline must include for reproducibility. NO tenant_id/run_id field here — tenant scoping is contextual (ADR-0024(e)).

kind.$comment: Evidence component class (ALG §3.7-3 snapshot+citation+timestamp+client-code-version+asset-hash decomposed into the pipeline's reproducibility inputs/outputs). Enum widening/narrowing = major (ADR-0012).

content_hash.$comment: sha256:-prefixed lowercase-hex content hash of the referenced evidence artifact — the tamper-evidence anchor. NO raw content inline (H10).

ref.$comment: Content-addressed object reference to where the evidence artifact is stored (ref ONLY — query/fragment forbidden, ADR-0024 R9-5).

entries.$comment: Ordered evidence entries (order is reorder/splice-evident via manifest_hash).

manifest_hash.$comment: Hash over the canonical serialization of the ordered entries[] (reuse saena_domain.audit canonical hashing — no new hashing rule). Any reorder/splice/mutation of entries changes this hash (E5 tamper-evidence).
